fields.name: LATENCY
fields.description: Latency These bits represent the ratio between the AHB hclk1 clock period and the memory access time. Access to the bit can be secured by RCC SYSCLKSEC. When secure, a non-secure read/write access is RAZ/WI. It does not generate an illegal access interrupt. This bit can be protected against unprivileged access when secure with SPRIV or when non-secure with NSPRIV. ... Note: Before entering Stop 1 mode software must set wait state latency to at least 1.

fields.name: PRFTEN
fields.description: Prefetch enable This bit enables the prefetch buffer in the embedded memory. This bit can be protected against unprivileged access by NSPRIV.

fields.name: LPM
fields.description: Low-power read mode This bit puts the memory in low-power read mode. Access to the bit can be secured by PWR LPMSEC. When secure, a non-secure read/write access is RAZ/WI. It does not generate an illegal access interrupt. This bit can be protected against unprivileged access when secure with SPRIV or when non-secure with NSPRIV. This bit can’t be written when a program or erase operation is busy (BSY = 1) or when the write buffer is not empty (WDW = 1). Changing this bit while a program or erase operation is busy (BSY = 1) is rejected.

fields.name: PDREQ1
fields.description: Bank 1 power-down mode request This bit is write-protected with FLASH_PDKEY1R. This bit requests bank 1 to enter power-down mode. When bank 1 enters power-down mode, this bit is cleared by hardware and the PDKEY1R is locked.

fields.name: PDREQ2
fields.description: Bank 2 power-down mode request This bit is write-protected with FLASH_PDKEY2R. This bit requests bank 2 to enter power-down mode. When bank 2 enters power-down mode, this bit is cleared by hardware and the PDKEY2R is locked.

fields.name: SLEEP_PD
fields.description: memory power-down mode during Sleep mode This bit determines whether the memory is in power-down mode or Idle mode when the device is in Sleep mode. Access to the bit can be secured by PWR LPMSEC. When secure, a non-secure read/write access is RAZ/WI. It does not generate an illegal access interrupt. This bit can be protected against unprivileged access when secure with SPRIV or when non-secure with NSPRIV. The must not be put in power-down while a program or an erase operation is ongoing.

fields.name: EOP
fields.description: Non-secure end of operation This bit is set by hardware when one or more memory non-secure operation (program/erase) has been completed successfully. This bit is set only if the non-secure end of operation interrupts are enabled (EOPIE = 1 in NSCR1). This bit is cleared by writing�1.

fields.name: OPERR
fields.description: Non-secure operation error This bit is set by hardware when a memory non-secure operation (program/erase) completes unsuccessfully. This bit is set only if non-secure error interrupts are enabled (NSERRIE = 1). This bit is cleared by writing 1.

fields.name: PROGERR
fields.description: Non-secure programming error This bit is set by hardware when a non-secure quad-word address to be programmed contains a value different from all 1 before programming, except if the data to write is all 0. This bit is cleared by writing 1.

fields.name: WRPERR
fields.description: Non-secure write protection error This bit is set by hardware when a non-secure address to be erased/programmed belongs to a write-protected part (by WRP or HDP) of the memory. This bit is cleared by writing 1. Refer to Section�7.3.10: memory errors flags for full conditions of error flag setting.

fields.name: PGAERR
fields.description: Non-secure programming alignment error This bit is set by hardware when the first word to be programmed is not aligned with a quad-word address, or the second, third or forth word does not belong to the same quad-word address. This bit is cleared by writing 1.

fields.name: SIZERR
fields.description: Non-secure size error This bit is set by hardware when the size of the access is a byte or half-word during a non-secure program sequence. Only quad-word programming is allowed by means of successive word accesses. This bit is cleared by writing 1.

fields.name: PGSERR
fields.description: Non-secure programming sequence error This bit is set by hardware when programming sequence is not correct. It is cleared by writing 1. Refer to Section�7.3.10: memory errors flags for full conditions of error flag setting.

fields.name: OPTWERR
fields.description: Option write error  This bit is set by hardware when the options bytes are written with an invalid configuration or when modifying options in RDP level 2.. It is cleared by writing 1. Refer to Section�7.3.10: memory errors flags for full conditions of error flag setting.

fields.name: BSY
fields.description: Non-secure busy This indicates that a memory secure or non-secure operation is in progress. This bit is set at the beginning of a operation and reset when the operation finishes or when an error occurs.

fields.name: WDW
fields.description: Non-secure wait data to write This bit indicates that the memory write buffer has been written by a secure or non-secure operation. It is set when the first data is stored in the buffer and cleared when the write is performed in the memory.

fields.name: OEM1LOCK
fields.description: OEM1 key RDP lock This bit indicates that the OEM1 key read during the OBL is not virgin. When set, the OEM1 key RDP lock mechanism is active.

fields.name: OEM2LOCK
fields.description: OEM2 key RDP lock This bit indicates that the OEM2 key read during the OBL is not virgin. When set, the OEM2 key RDP lock mechanism is active.

fields.name: PD1
fields.description: Bank 1 in power-down mode This bit indicates that the Flash memory bank 1 is in power-down state. It is reset when bank 1 is in normal mode or being awaken.

fields.name: PD2
fields.description: Bank 2 in power-down mode This bit indicates that the Flash memory bank 2 is in power-down state. It is reset when bank 2 is in normal mode or being awaken.

fields.name: EOP
fields.description: Secure end of operation This bit is set by hardware when one or more memory secure operation (program/erase) has been completed successfully. This bit is set only if the secure end of operation interrupts are enabled (EOPIE = 1 in SECCR1). This bit is cleared by writing�1.

fields.name: OPERR
fields.description: Secure operation error This bit is set by hardware when a memory secure operation (program/erase) completes unsuccessfully. This bit is set only if secure error interrupts are enabled (SECERRIE = 1). This bit is cleared by writing 1.

fields.name: PROGERR
fields.description: Secure programming error This bit is set by hardware when a secure quad-word address to be programmed contains a value different from all 1 before programming, except if the data to write is all 0. This bit is cleared by writing 1.

fields.name: WRPERR
fields.description: Secure write protection error This bit is set by hardware when an secure address to be erased/programmed belongs to a write-protected part (by WRP or HDP) of the memory. This bit is cleared by writing 1. Refer to Section�7.3.10: memory errors flags for full conditions of error flag setting.

fields.name: PGAERR
fields.description: Secure programming alignment error This bit is set by hardware when the first word to be programmed is not aligned with a quad-word address, or the second, third or forth word does not belong to the same quad-word address.This bit is cleared by writing 1.

fields.name: SIZERR
fields.description: Secure size error This bit is set by hardware when the size of the access is a byte or half-word during a secure program sequence. Only quad-word programming is allowed by means of successive word accesses.This bit is cleared by writing 1.

fields.name: PGSERR
fields.description: Secure programming sequence error This bit is set by hardware when programming sequence is not correct. It is cleared by writing 1. Refer to Section�7.3.10: memory errors flags for full conditions of error flag setting.

fields.name: BSY
fields.description: Secure busy This bit indicates that a memory secure or non-secure operation is in progress. This is set on the beginning of a operation and reset when the operation finishes or when an error occurs.

fields.name: WDW
fields.description: Secure wait data to write This bit indicates that the memory write buffer has been written by a secure or non-secure operation. It is set when the first data is stored in the buffer and cleared when the write is performed in the memory.

fields.name: PG
fields.description: Non-secure programming

fields.name: PER
fields.description: Non-secure page erase

fields.name: MER1
fields.description: Non-secure bank 1 mass erase This bit triggers the bank 1 non-secure mass erase (all bank 1 user pages) when set.

fields.name: PNB
fields.description: Non-secure page number selection These bits select the page to erase. ...

fields.name: BKER
fields.description: Non-secure bank selection for page erase

fields.name: BWR
fields.description: Non-secure burst write programming mode When set, this bit selects the burst write programming mode.

fields.name: MER2
fields.description: Non-secure bank 2 mass erase This bit triggers the bank 2 non-secure mass erase (all bank 2 user pages) when set.

fields.name: STRT
fields.description: Non-secure start This bit triggers a non-secure erase operation when set. If MER1, MER2 and PER bits are reset and the STRT bit is set, the PGSERR bit in FLASH_NSSR is set (this condition is forbidden). This bit is set only by software and is cleared when the BSY bit is cleared in FLASH_NSSR.

fields.name: OPTSTRT
fields.description: Options modification start This bit triggers an options operation when set. It can not be written if OPTLOCK bit is set. This bit is set only by software, and is cleared when the BSY bit is cleared in FLASH_NSSR.

fields.name: EOPIE
fields.description: Non-secure end of operation interrupt enable This bit enables the interrupt generation when the EOP bit in the FLASH_NSSR is set to 1.

fields.name: ERRIE
fields.description: Non-secure error interrupt enable This bit enables the interrupt generation when the OPERR bit in the FLASH_NSSR is set to 1.

fields.name: OBL_LAUNCH
fields.description: Force the option byte loading When set to 1, this bit forces the option byte reloading. This bit is cleared only when the option byte loading is complete. It cannot be written if OPTLOCK is set.

fields.name: OPTLOCK
fields.description: Option lock This bit is set only. When set, all bits concerning user options in FLASH_NSCR register are locked. This bit is cleared by hardware after detecting the unlock sequence. The LOCK bit in the FLASH_NSCR must be cleared before doing the unlock sequence for OPTLOCK bit. In case of an unsuccessful unlock operation, this bit remains set until the next reset.

fields.name: LOCK
fields.description: Non-secure lock This bit is set only. When set, the FLASH_NSCR register is locked. It is cleared by hardware after detecting the unlock sequence in FLASH_NSKEYR register. In case of an unsuccessful unlock operation, this bit remains set until the next system reset.

fields.name: PG
fields.description: Secure programming

fields.name: PER
fields.description: Secure page erase

fields.name: MER1
fields.description: Secure bank 1 mass erase This bit triggers the bank 1 secure mass erase (all bank 1 user pages) when set.

fields.name: PNB
fields.description: Secure page number selection These bits select the page to erase: ...

fields.name: BKER
fields.description: Secure bank selection for page erase

fields.name: BWR
fields.description: Secure burst write programming mode When set, this bit selects the burst write programming mode.

fields.name: MER2
fields.description: Secure bank 2 mass erase This bit triggers the bank 2 secure mass erase (all bank 2 user pages) when set.

fields.name: STRT
fields.description: Secure start This bit triggers a secure erase operation when set. If MER1, MER2 and PER bits are reset and the STRT bit is set, the PGSERR in the FLASH_SECSR is set (this condition is forbidden). This bit is set only by software and is cleared when the BSY bit is cleared in FLASH_SECSR.

fields.name: EOPIE
fields.description: Secure End of operation interrupt enable This bit enables the interrupt generation when the EOP bit in the FLASH_SECSR is set to 1.

fields.name: ERRIE
fields.description: Secure error interrupt enable

fields.name: RDERRIE
fields.description: Secure PCROP read error interrupt enable

fields.name: INV
fields.description: Flash memory security state invert This bit inverts the Flash memory security state.

fields.name: LOCK
fields.description: Secure lock This bit is set only. When set, the FLASH_SECCR register is locked. It is cleared by hardware after detecting the unlock sequence in FLASH_SECKEYR register. In case of an unsuccessful unlock operation, this bit remains set until the next system reset.

fields.name: ADDR_ECC
fields.description: ECC fail address This field indicates which address is concerned by the ECC error correction or by the double ECC error detection. The address is given relative to base address, from offset 0x0�0000 to 0xF�FFF0. Note that bit 19 is reserved on STM32WBAxEx devices.

fields.name: BK_ECC
fields.description: ECC fail bank

fields.name: SYSF_ECC
fields.description: System memory ECC fail This bit indicates that the ECC error correction or double ECC error detection is located in the system memory.

fields.name: ECCIE
fields.description: ECC correction interrupt enable This bit enables the interrupt generation when the ECCC bit in the ECCR register is set.

fields.name: ECCC
fields.description: ECC correction This bit is set by hardware when one ECC error has been detected and corrected (only if ECCC and ECCD were previously cleared). An interrupt is generated if ECCIE is set. This bit is cleared by writing 1.

fields.name: ECCD
fields.description: ECC detection This bit is set by hardware when two ECC errors have been detected (only if ECCC and ECCD were previously cleared). When this bit is set, a NMI is generated. This bit is cleared by writing 1.

fields.name: ADDR_OP
fields.description: Interrupted operation address This field indicates which address in the memory was accessed when reset occurred. The address is given relative to the base address, from offset 0x0�0000 to 0xF�FFF0. Note that bit 19 is reserved on STM32WBAxEx devices.

fields.name: BK_OP
fields.description: Interrupted operation bank This bit indicates which Flash memory bank was accessed when reset occurred

fields.name: SYSF_OP
fields.description: Operation in system memory interrupted This bit indicates that the reset occurred during an operation in the system memory.

fields.name: CODE_OP
fields.description: memory operation code This field indicates which memory operation has been interrupted by a system reset:

fields.name: PS
fields.description: Program suspend request

fields.name: ES
fields.description: Erase suspend request

fields.name: PS
fields.description: Program suspend request

fields.name: ES
fields.description: Erase suspend request

fields.name: RDP
fields.description: Readout protection level Others: Level 1 (memories readout protection active) Note: Refer to Section�7.6.2: Readout protection (RDP) for more details.

fields.name: BOR_LEV
fields.description: BOR reset level These bits contain the V<sub>DD</sub> supply level threshold that activates/releases the reset.

fields.name: nRST_STOP
fields.description: Reset generation in Stop mode

fields.name: nRST_STDBY
fields.description: Reset generation in Standby mode

fields.name: nRST_SHDW
fields.description: Reset generation in Shutdown mode

fields.name: SRAM1_RST
fields.description: SRAM1 erase upon system reset

fields.name: IWDG_SW
fields.description: Independent watchdog enable selection

fields.name: IWDG_STOP
fields.description: Independent watchdog counter freeze in Stop mode

fields.name: IWDG_STDBY
fields.description: Independent watchdog counter freeze in Standby mode

fields.name: WWDG_SW
fields.description: Window watchdog selection

fields.name: SWAP_BANK
fields.description: Swap banks

fields.name: DUALBANK
fields.description: Dual-bank on 1-Mbyte and 512-Kbyte Flash memory devices

fields.name: SRAM2_PE
fields.description: SRAM2 parity check enable

fields.name: SRAM2_RST
fields.description: SRAM2 erase when system reset

fields.name: nSWBOOT0
fields.description: Software BOOT0

fields.name: nBOOT0
fields.description: nBOOT0 option bit

fields.name: IO_VDD_HSLV
fields.description: High-speed IO at low VDD voltage configuration bit This bit can be set only with VDD below 2.5V

fields.name: IO_VDDIO2_HSLV
fields.description: High-speed IO at low VDDIO2 voltage configuration bit This bit can be set only with VDDIO2 below 2.5 V.

fields.name: TZEN
fields.description: Global TrustZone security enable

fields.name: NSBOOTADD0
fields.description: Non-secure boot base address 0 This address is only used when TZEN = 0. The non-secure boot memory address can be programmed to any address in the valid address range (see Table 28: Boot space versus RDP protection) with a granularity of 128 bytes. These bits correspond to address [31:7]. The NSBOOTADD0 option bytes are selected following the BOOT0 pin or NSWBOOT0 state. Examples: NSBOOTADD0[24:0] = 0x0100000: Boot from memory (0x0800 0000) NSBOOTADD0[24:0] = 0x017F100: Boot from system memory bootloader (0x0BF8 8000) NSBOOTADD0[24:0] = 0x0400200: Boot from SRAM2 on S-Bus (0x2001 0000)

fields.name: NSBOOTADD1
fields.description: Non-secure boot address 1 This address is only used when TZEN = 0. The non-secure boot memory address can be programmed to any address in the valid address range (see Table 28: Boot space versus RDP protection) with a granularity of 128 bytes. These bits correspond to address [31:7]. The NSBOOTADD0 option bytes are selected following the BOOT0 pin or NSWBOOT0 state.  Examples: NSBOOTADD1[24:0] = 0x0100000: Boot from memory (0x0800 0000) NSBOOTADD1[24:0] = 0x017F100: Boot from system memory bootloader (0x0BF8 8000) NSBOOTADD1[24:0] = 0x0400200: Boot from SRAM2 (0x2001 0000)

fields.name: BOOT_LOCK
fields.description: Boot lock This lock is only used when TZEN = 0. When set, the boot is always forced to base address value programmed in SECBOOTADD0[24:0] option bytes whatever the boot selection option. When set, this bit can only be cleared by an RDP regression level 1 to level 0.

fields.name: SECBOOTADD0
fields.description: Secure boot base address 0 This address is only used when TZEN = 1. The secure boot memory address can be programmed to any address in the valid address range (see Table�28: Boot space versus RDP protection) with a granularity of 128 bytes. This bits correspond to address [31:7] The SECBOOTADD0 option bytes are selected following the BOOT0 pin or NSWBOOT0 state.  Examples: SECBOOTADD0[24:0] = 0x018 0000: Boot from secure user memory (0x0C00 0000) SECBOOTADD0[24:0] = 0x01F F000: Boot from RSS system memory (0x0FF8 0000) SECBOOTADD0[24:0] = 0x060 0000: Boot from secure SRAM1 on S-Bus (0x3000 0000)

fields.name: SECWM1_PSTRT
fields.description: Start page of first secure area This field contains the first page of the secure area in bank 1.

fields.name: SECWM1_PEND
fields.description: End page of first secure area This field contains the last page of the secure area in bank 1.

fields.name: HDP1_PEND
fields.description: End page of first hide protection area This field contains the last page of the HDP area in bank 1.

fields.name: HDP1EN
fields.description: Hide protection first area enable

registers.name: WRP1AR
registers.description: Flash WRP bank 1 area A address register
registers.fieldset_description: FLASH WRP1 area A address register

fields.name: WRP1A_PSTRT
fields.description: WPR area A start page This field contains the first page of the WPR area A. Note that bit 6 is reserved on STM32WBAxEx devices.

fields.name: WRP1A_PEND
fields.description: WPR area A end page This field contains the last page of the WPR area A. Note that bit 22 is reserved on STM32WBAxEx devices.

fields.name: UNLOCK
fields.description: WPR area A unlock

registers.name: WRP1BR
registers.description: Flash WRP bank 1 area B address register
registers.fieldset_description: FLASH WRP1 area B address register

fields.name: WRP1B_PSTRT
fields.description: WRP area B start page This field contains the first page of the WRP area B. Note that bit 6 is reserved on STM32WBAxEx devices.

fields.name: WRP1B_PEND
fields.description: WRP area B end page This field contains the last page of the WRP area B. Note that bit 22 is reserved on STM32WBAxEx devices.

fields.name: UNLOCK
fields.description: WPR area B unlock

fields.name: SECWM2_PSTRT
fields.description: WRP area B start page This field contains the first page of the secure area in bank 2.

fields.name: SECWM2_PEND
fields.description: End page of secure area This field contains the last page of the secure area in bank 2.

fields.name: HDP2_PEND
fields.description: Bank 2 end page of secure hide protection area This field contains the last page of the secure HDP area in bank 2.

fields.name: HDP2EN
fields.description: Bank 2 secure Hide protection area enable

registers.name: WRP2AR
registers.description: Flash WRP bank 2 area A address register
registers.fieldset_description: FLASH WPR2 area A address register

fields.name: WRP2A_PSTRT
fields.description: WRP bank 2 area A start page This field contains the first page of the WRP bank 2 area A.

fields.name: WRP2A_PEND
fields.description: WRP bank 2 area A end page This field contains the last page of the WRP bank 2 area A.

fields.name: UNLOCK
fields.description: WPR bank 2 area A unlock

registers.name: WRP2BR
registers.description: Flash WRP bank 2 area B address register
registers.fieldset_description: FLASH WPR2 area B address register

fields.name: WRP2B_PSTRT
fields.description: WRP bank 2 area B start page This field contains the first page of the WRP bank 2 area B.

fields.name: WRP2B_PEND
fields.description: WRP bank 2 area B end page This field contains the last page of the WRP bank 2 area B.

fields.name: UNLOCK
fields.description: WPR bank 2 area B unlock

registers.name: SECBB1R
registers.description: Flash bank 1 secure block based register 1

registers.name: SECBB2R
registers.description: Flash bank 2 secure block based register 1

fields.name: HDP1_ACCDIS
fields.description: HDP1 area access disable When set, this bit is only cleared by a system reset.

fields.name: HDP2_ACCDIS
fields.description: HDP2 area access disable When set, this bit is only cleared by a system reset.

fields.name: SPRIV
fields.description: Privileged protection for secure registers This bit is secure write protected. It can only be written by a secure privileged access when TrustZone is enabled (TZEN�=�1).

fields.name: NSPRIV
fields.description: Privileged protection for non-secure registers

registers.name: PRIVBB1R
registers.description: Flash bank 1 privilege block based register 1

registers.name: PRIVBB2R
registers.description: Flash bank 2 privilege block based register 1

variants.name: NoFlashInt
variants.description: No Flash operation interrupted by previous reset

variants.name: B_0x55
variants.description: Level 0.5 (readout protection not active, only non-secure debug access is possible). Only available when TrustZone is active (TZEN=1)
